name: JLPT N5 (4 Months)
description: If your goal is to pass the JLPT N5 in 4 months, this roadmap provides a daily schedule to get you there with 1.5–2 hours of daily study using Genki, Anki, and free resources.

tasks.title: Review old vocabulary (Anki)
tasks.description:
Open your Anki application and complete all your due reviews for the day. 

**Tips:**
- Don't skip days, as reviews pile up quickly!
- Be honest when grading yourself (Again/Hard/Good/Easy).
- Speak the words out loud to build muscle memory.

tasks.title: Learn Hiragana A-K rows & 20 words
tasks.description:
Time to study your characters! 

**Instructions:**
1. Use a spaced-repetition app or physical flashcards.
2. Practice writing them down on paper to memorize stroke order.
3. Try to read small words using these characters.

*Consistency is key to mastering the alphabets!*

tasks.title: Learn Greetings
tasks.description:
This task focuses on: **Learn Greetings**. 

Take your time to understand the concepts thoroughly. Make sure you have a quiet environment, take notes if necessary, and don't hesitate to review previous materials if you feel stuck. 

You've got this! Keep pushing forward!

tasks.title: Kanji (5 words)
tasks.description:
Kanji is the backbone of Japanese reading. 

**Instructions:**
- Learn the core meanings and the most common readings (Onyomi and Kunyomi).
- Practice writing the character.
- Study vocabulary words that use this Kanji in context.

Don't try to memorize every single reading, focus on the vocabulary!

tasks.title: Hiragana S-T rows
tasks.description:
Time to study your characters! 

**Instructions:**
1. Use a spaced-repetition app or physical flashcards.
2. Practice writing them down on paper to memorize stroke order.
3. Try to read small words using these characters.

*Consistency is key to mastering the alphabets!*

tasks.title: 20 words
tasks.description:
Expand your vocabulary by learning these new words. 

**Action Items:**
- Add these words to your Anki deck if they aren't already there.
- Listen to native audio for pronunciation.
- Try writing one simple sentence for each word to solidify it in your memory.

tasks.description:

**Instructions:**
- Learn the core meanings and the most common readings (Onyomi and Kunyomi).

tasks.title: Grammar (は, の, か, これ, あれ)
tasks.description:
Grammar builds the structure of your sentences. 

**Study Guide:**
- Read the grammar explanation carefully in your textbook.
- Review all example sentences.
- Complete the corresponding workbook exercises.

*Tip: Don't just read; try speaking the example sentences out loud!*
